steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student must emphasize a similarity between the brown violetear and the little hermit. The decisive clue in the notes is that both birds are described as having a "black" bill, making bill color the one trait they share.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'While each exhibits unique traits of its own, the brown violetear and the little hermit both have black bills.': Names both birds and identifies their shared trait (black bills) while acknowledging each has distinct features. ✓
  • Choice B — 'The little hermit is a small hummingbird identifiable by its white central tail feathers and its long, black, curved bill.': Describes only the little hermit; never mentions the brown violetear, so no similarity is drawn. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Though they share several traits in common, the brown violetear is larger than the little hermit.': Focuses on a difference (size) rather than emphasizing a similarity; the phrase "share several traits" is vague and unsupported by the notes. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Birds of Northern South America is an expertly compiled guidebook by ornithologists Robin Restall, Clemencia Rodner, and Miguel Lentino.': Describes the guidebook, not either bird; draws no comparison at all. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
